steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Let $u = x^2$ to turn the quartic into a quadratic: $x^4 - 2x^2 - 15$ becomes $u^2 - 2u - 15$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Factor the quadratic $u^2 - 2u - 15$. Find two numbers that multiply to $-15$ and add to $-2$: those numbers are $+3$ and $-5$. So $u^2 - 2u - 15 = (u + 3)(u - 5)$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Verify: $(x^2 + 3)(x^2 - 5) = x^4 - 5x^2 + 3x^2 - 15 = x^4 - 2x^2 - 15$ ✓. This matches choice B, so B is correct.
  • $(x^2+1)(x^2-15)$ expands to $x^4 - 14x^2 - 15$, so the middle term is wrong.
  • $(x^2+3)(x^2-5)$ expands to $x^4 - 2x^2 - 15$, matching the original expression.
  • $(x^2+5)(x^2-3)$ expands to $x^4 + 2x^2 - 15$, so the middle term has the wrong sign.
  • $(x^2+15)(x^2-1)$ expands to $x^4 + 14x^2 - 15$, so the middle term is wrong.
